title: Evaversal Laws of Physics
wikitext:
[]: Evaversal Laws of Physics             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Thu Dec 04, 2008 4:02 pm I've been thinking for quite a while that a set of physical laws governing the Evaverse would be quite useful for reference when it comes to random quantum magic/LnY self-propulsion/AT Field/energy source types of discussions. After all, there's a field of science in NGE known as "metaphysical biology" which is the study of the soul, and we know that it's no philosophical waffle (you actually get useful jobs out of it, like Fuyutsuki did), so why not Evaversal Physics? For example, any {AT Field-related} force, given that the AT Field concerned is strong enough to exert a force to begin with, completely overrides any other physical force (i.e. Angels floating; Rei's quantum appearances) [apart from the LnY self-propulsion forces, which are stronger than them; and other AT Fields, which neutralize depending on Field strength] and can be maintained indefinitely as long as the AT Field exists (you don't see Angels randomly falling out of the sky, unless they mean to, or they're dead). PWM can produce non-PWM matter (Lilith's offspring, Matariel crying acid, Rei bleeding) but not vice versa. To produce more PWM you need some PWM to begin with. (Adam's offspring etc.) Here's one I'm considering putting in: Mental effort in performing an AT-Field related physical action is equal to physical effort such an act would take, so that an effort is needed for Shinji and Asuka to open Gaghiel's mouth at the last moment. This would also account for things such as contamination of the pilot's mental state and such. Any more?

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Thu Dec 04, 2008 9:55 pm Plenty more. Just remember to cross reference and examine the differences between old threads. S2 engine is a virtually unlimited energy source (there's something special about that double helix), likely replenish able, however for high output in a short period of time, there must be a cooldown period or what seems to be one anyway (i'm thinking 2I here with adam and the AATF, but perhaps this is an invalid point due to adam being a seed of life and all). It's interesting that non-PWM matter can be changed into PWM matter thus making it insoluble... (gendo's arm absorption) There's also quite a bit from this old thread on some specifics in metaphysical biology. :P Other things can be found from other threads. Is there any good one on the quantum magic thing?

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Thu Dec 04, 2008 10:04 pm "Piloting skills are proportional to the amount of psychological trauma incurred by the pilot during childhood."

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Thu Dec 04, 2008 10:49 pm ... >.> Try keeping it from looking like this , please...

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Fri Dec 05, 2008 9:07 am Just had a look at how that old thread ended...and hope sorely that Mr. Tines won't do the same thing to this one... Just remember, this isn't real physics. This is Evaversal physics. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Fri Dec 05, 2008 1:50 pm Count A'ight wrote: "Piloting skills are proportional to the amount of psychological trauma incurred by the pilot during childhood."

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Fri Dec 05, 2008 2:46 pm Fact: AT Fields diffuse over a certain range, extending beyond the body (which is the AT Field's basic form). Hypothesis: AT Fields act through the subconscious, remaining partially active while a being is awake, but extending fully when unconscious, without the conscious mind to rein them in and focus them. By "extending" I mean they expand over a certain range, diffusing to fill the space around the individual. In such a state they rarely exert strong physical forces, but have evident and significant psychological effects that may influence the mental states of those around them. Hence a possible explanation for Shinji meeting Touji on the hell train, who in reality was in the next bed; for Yui-sama only going berserk when Shinji was unconscious (or barely conscious) in the entry plug, letting her detect his plight, etc. How's that sound?

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Fri Dec 05, 2008 3:41 pm AyrYntake wrote: Yui-sama only going berserk when Shinji was unconscious (or barely conscious) in the entry plug

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Fri Dec 05, 2008 3:45 pm Nope. He was unconscious (monitors not responding) during the berserker battle with Sachiel, and at the brink of consciousness when trapped inside Leliel by the time Eva-01 broke out. As for Zeruel, we don't know what happened because no monitoring was showed to us when Eva-01 went berserk, but there's no proof that Shinji wasn't absorbed into the LCL quite soon after Eva-01's reactivation. Conscious, but merged with the host body?

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Fri Dec 05, 2008 4:10 pm AyrYntake wrote: Nope. He was unconscious (monitors not responding) during the berserker battle with Sachiel

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Fri Dec 05, 2008 4:20 pm Were he awake after he'd been smashed into a building by Sachiel, he'd have gotten up and continued fighting immediately. The fact that there's a huge gap in between the crash and the reactivation, which is a clear sign of Shinji's lack of consciousness. Also, we're shown a zooming close-up of Shinji's face before the reactivation scene, and he's basically sitting there expressionless and dazed. The Zeruel battle is a hard one to define; after all, Yui herself was under attack during that sequence so it's not as if she wouldn't have taken over anyway. One thing I might mention is that we notice the walls of the entry plug cracking under Zeruel's onslaught; my mere speculation is that if LCL were allowed to flow directly between the pilot and the body of the Eva unit, the connection effect of that would have some sort of contribution towards her awakening. Feel free to attack my stance on that one.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Fri Dec 05, 2008 4:25 pm AyrYntake wrote: Were he awake after he'd been smashed into a building by Sachiel, he'd have gotten up and continued fighting immediately.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Fri Dec 05, 2008 4:30 pm Is this some personal grudge against me you have, that you attack the arguments I consider sound and leave the ones I consider shaky? Honestly, what'd I ever do to you? Having your eyes open does not equal being conscious. Especially when you're immobile for at least half a minute or so. The other times we've seen him "sitting there like a pussy", he was at least cowering in fear and you could hear his distorted breathing, as was the case with Shamshel and Bardiel.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Fri Dec 05, 2008 4:33 pm AyrYntake wrote: Is this some personal grudge against me you have, that you attack the arguments I consider sound and leave the ones I consider shaky? Honestly, what'd I ever do to you?
